questions.question: Compared with a flat inspection mirror of the same size, a convex inspection mirror:
options: Shows a magnified image with a smaller field of view
Covers a wider field of view with a reduced image size
Eliminates ghost images from the glass front surface
Shows true size and shape of the surface
questions.explanation: A convex mirror diverges the reflected rays, so it takes in a wider area but renders everything smaller — useful for scanning a large cavity before returning with a flat mirror for detail.

questions.question: A procedure written in footcandles requires 50 fc for general examination, but your calibrated meter reads in lux. The minimum acceptable reading is about:
options: 50 lux
110 lux
540 lux
1080 lux
questions.explanation: One footcandle is about 10.76 lux, so 50 fc converts to roughly 538 lux at the inspection surface.

questions.question: Your acceptance call on a stainless weld depends on judging heat-tint colors. Which property of your light source matters most?
options: The lumens rating printed on the package
The beam angle
The battery chemistry
The color rendering index (CRI)
questions.explanation: CRI describes how faithfully a source renders colors compared with a reference; a low-CRI lamp can shift or wash out oxide and heat-tint colors and lead to a wrong call, no matter how bright it is.

questions.question: Which direction of view puts the wall of a straight bore directly in front of the borescope optics as the tip passes alongside it?
options: Forward (0 degrees)
Forward-oblique (about 45 degrees)
Retro (about 120 degrees)
Side (90 degrees)
questions.explanation: A 90-degree side view faces the surface beside the tip, which is exactly where a bore or cylinder wall sits; forward views look ahead and retro views look back over the scope's shoulder.

questions.question: Total internal reflection keeps light inside an optical fiber because:
options: The cladding absorbs stray light before it escapes
The core's refractive index is higher than the cladding's, so light within the acceptance angle reflects at the boundary
The fiber is coated with a silvered mirror layer
The light travels through a hollow vacuum core
questions.explanation: When light in the higher-index core strikes the lower-index cladding beyond the critical angle, it reflects completely back into the core — repeating at every bounce, which lets the fiber carry light around bends.

questions.question: Which handling rule protects a fiberscope's image bundle?
options: Store it coiled as tightly as possible to protect the tip
Withdraw it from the part by pulling hard on the insertion tube
Never bend it tighter than the manufacturer's minimum bend radius
Wipe the objective lens with a dry shop rag after each use
questions.explanation: Bending tighter than the minimum bend radius snaps fibers, and every broken fiber is a permanent black dot in the image; scopes are stored loosely coiled and handled by the body, with optics cleaned using lens-safe materials.

questions.question: A vessel internal examination can be done by sending a technician inside on a ladder or by lowering a pan-tilt-zoom camera. The strongest argument for the RVI option is that it:
options: Eliminates the confined-space entry while still meeting resolution and lighting requirements
Is exempt from the procedure's examination requirements
Never needs its lighting verified
Produces images that are automatically code-compliant
questions.explanation: RVI removes the personnel hazard — confined-space entry, dose, elevated work — but the examination requirements still apply: demonstrated resolution equivalence, verified lighting, and documented locations.

questions.question: Which gauge is specifically designed to compare surface porosity size and measure undercut depth against workmanship limits?
options: Hi-Lo gauge
Taper gauge
V-WAC gauge
Telescoping gauge
questions.explanation: The V-WAC gauge is a workmanship gauge that reads undercut depth and crown height and carries comparison features for judging surface porosity diameter against acceptance limits.

questions.question: A dial indicator on a magnetic base rests its plunger on a shaft that is slowly rotated. The total sweep of the needle represents the shaft's:
options: Total indicator runout (TIR)
Surface roughness
Hardness
Thread pitch
questions.explanation: The indicator measures change in position: as the shaft turns, the difference between the highest and lowest needle readings is the total indicator runout.

questions.question: Before an automated machine-vision system is trusted to make accept/reject calls on a production line, it should be:
options: Run at reduced speed for the first week
Approved by the camera manufacturer only
Set to flag every part for manual review permanently
Validated against known reference standards or seeded-defect samples, then re-verified on a schedule
questions.explanation: An automated system finds only what it is set up to find, so its detection capability must be proven with known standards before service and periodically re-verified, with humans reviewing flagged or borderline results.
